prompt:
Our gateway pipeline (middleware.ts) is sync-only and errors just explode out of run() — every team wraps their middleware in try/catch and invents their own 500 page. I want the Express model, but awaitable.

Async middleware: any middleware may be async / return a promise; the pipeline awaits it. next() returns a promise that settles when everything downstream is done, so `await next(); measure()` works for timing middleware. run(ctx) stays awaitable and still resolves to the same ctx object — every call site in the gateway already does `await pipeline.run(ctx)` (the regression tests do too), and sync-only pipelines must behave byte-for-byte as before.

Error handlers, registered through the same use() and told apart by arity exactly like Express: a 3-arg function (err, ctx, next) is an error handler; regular traffic never touches them. When any middleware throws or rejects — including on the unwind after its await next() — remaining REGULAR middleware are skipped and handlers get the error in registration order. Inside a handler: finish without calling next() = error handled, run resolves; next(newErr) = forward to the next handler; throwing inside a handler forwards the NEW error to the next handler; next() with no arg = declare it handled and stop the handler chain. Nothing left to handle it -> run rejects with whatever error was last in play. And handlers must run exactly once per error — I don't want a retry storm because someone chained next(err) at the end.

test_error_async.ts is the acceptance contract, test_middleware.ts pins today's semantics. Both are read-only. node --test test_middleware.ts test_error_async.ts
